title: stenaria rupicola
intro: Stenaria rupicola, also known as False Puccoon or False Indian Paintbrush, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its small, yellow, and orange flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Stenaria rupicola include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Stenaria rupicola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice for natural remedies.
